# HSREP — Health System Resilience & Economic Protection

> HSREP is an independent policy-research and advocacy platform making the case that health-system resilience is economic protection: when health systems hold under shock, households, institutions, and public trust hold with them. It publishes evidence-based arguments, pairs each with usable media and advocacy tools, and turns them into structured professional discussion. Founded 2026 by Md Shafaat Ali Choyon (MBA, MCIM, MPH, CHES®). Independent and non-partisan; all figures carry their source and methodology.

## Core proposition

- Health protection is economic infrastructure, not a residual budget line. Structural underinvestment in health systems produces the same kind of failure across very different economies — demonstrated across the United States and Bangladesh.

## Platform

- [Homepage](https://hsraep.org/): The platform's thesis, current season, findings, and ways for professionals to engage.
- [Season 1](https://hsraep.org/season-1.html): A two-country public-health advocacy campaign (U.S. + Bangladesh) — one cumulative argument told across nine published pieces, reaching 57,274 people at $0 ad spend.
- [Methodology](https://hsraep.org/methodology.html): How Season 1's 57,274 organic impressions are measured and reconciled — per-platform definitions, the full breakdown, dashboard reconciliation (96.6% Facebook / 94.7% LinkedIn), limitations, and the complete downloadable dataset.
- [Articles](https://hsraep.org/articles.html): The full library of published arguments, each a complete advocacy package with evidence, media, and a response pathway.
- [Roundtable](https://hsraep.org/roundtable.html): Structured, time-boxed professional discussion tied to a specific finding. Roundtable No. 1 runs August 1–15, 2026.
- [Films & explainers](https://hsraep.org/films.html): Short and long-form video and infographic explainers of the arguments.
- [About](https://hsraep.org/about.html): Purpose, origin, founder, editorial standards, and corrections policy.

## Applied initiative

- [Prevention Adoption Initiative](https://hsraep.org/initiative.html): A proposed, in-development toolkit that closes the "Prevention Adoption Gap" — the distance between recommended and completed preventive care in safety-net settings — beginning with a proposed colorectal-cancer screening completion pilot in Southeast Michigan. Status: proposed and in development; nothing has been deployed. All operational designs are illustrative.

## Areas of focus (evergreen lenses)

- Household financial protection (medical debt, catastrophic cost)
- System resilience & preparedness (disaster readiness, continuity under shock)
- Health financing as investment (public health spending as economic infrastructure)
- Prevention & adoption (why proven preventive services stall at completion)
- Applied AI & health-system equity (predictive and agentic tools, explainable and fairness-audited)

## Key findings from Season 1

- Platform, not content, decides who sees a health argument — the same piece routed to opposite audiences on Facebook vs. LinkedIn.
- Tagging named officials is a repeatable ~3x reach amplifier, but concentrates a home-country audience rather than crossing borders.
- Algorithmic recommendation ("Suggested" traffic) is the gate to cross-border reach, achievable without ad spend.
- Sustained binational framing built a genuinely two-country audience by the campaign's close.
- Reach is fully sourced and reconciled: the 57,274 organic impressions are 36,997 Facebook views + 13,054 LinkedIn impressions + 7,223 Instagram native views, reconciled to the platform dashboards at 96.6% (Facebook) and 94.7% (LinkedIn). Full post-level dataset: https://hsraep.org/assets/HSR_Season1_Master_Dataset.xlsx

## Founder

- Md Shafaat Ali Choyon — growth, marketing and public-health strategist (MBA, MCIM, MPH, CHES®), 16+ years turning complex systems into measurable results. Founder site: https://shafaatalichoyon.com

## Contact

- Partnerships, media, and professional participation: via the platform's contact and roundtable pathways at https://hsraep.org/about.html
